prompt: Implement RMSNorm as a torch.nn.Module class named `RMSNorm` WITHOUT using torch.nn.RMSNorm. Constructor: RMSNorm(dim: int, eps: float = 1e-6). Forward: applies root-mean-square normalization across the last dim and multiplies by a learnable gain parameter of shape (dim,). The gain must be a torch.nn.Parameter named `weight` initialized to ones.

criteria.description: All pytest tests in unit_tests pass against the candidate implementation

unit_tests:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from solution import RMSNorm

def test_shape_preserved():
    rms = RMSNorm(dim=8)
    x = torch.randn(2, 4, 8)
    assert rms(x).shape == x.shape

def test_normalizes_to_unit_rms():
    rms = RMSNorm(dim=16, eps=1e-12)
    torch.manual_seed(0)
    x = torch.randn(32, 16) * 5.0  # large scale
    out = rms(x)
    actual_rms = out.pow(2).mean(dim=-1).sqrt()
    assert torch.allclose(actual_rms, torch.ones_like(actual_rms), atol=1e-4), actual_rms

def test_gain_is_learnable_parameter_named_weight():
    rms = RMSNorm(dim=12)
    assert isinstance(rms.weight, nn.Parameter)
    assert rms.weight.requires_grad
    assert rms.weight.shape == (12,)
    assert torch.allclose(rms.weight, torch.ones(12))

def test_eps_prevents_div_by_zero():
    rms = RMSNorm(dim=4, eps=1e-6)
    x = torch.zeros(1, 4)
    out = rms(x)
    assert torch.isfinite(out).all()
